title: दुर्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दुर्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीदुर्गासहस्रनामस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारायण ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहादुर्गा देवता।
श्रीदुर्गाप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

नारायण उवाच -

शतनामप्रवक्ष्यामि शृणु नारद तत्त्वतः।
सहस्रनामसंपूर्णं दुर्गायाः परमात्मनः॥

ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं दुं दुर्गायै नमः।

शिवा उमा रमा ब्राह्मी वरदा पुष्करेक्षणा।
गायत्री पार्वती चैव दुर्गा दुर्गार्तिनाशिनी॥१॥

जया चाद्या विषालाक्षी मुक्तिदा शुद्धरूपिणी।
महामाया महादेवी महालक्ष्मी मनोन्मनी॥२॥

महाशक्तिर्महोत्साहा महाबला महाप्रभा।
महाबुद्धिर्महासिद्धिर्महायोगीश्वरेश्वरी॥३॥

महातन्त्रा महामन्त्रा महायन्त्रा महासना।
महायागक्रमाराध्या महाभैरवपूजिता॥४॥

महेश्वरी महाकाली महाग्रासा महाशना।
अपर्णा चण्डिका चण्डमुण्डासुरनिषूदिनी॥५॥

महिषासुरनिर्नाशी भक्तानामभयप्रदा।
जयदा बुद्धिदा नित्या शर्वाणी शर्ववल्लभा॥६॥

सर्वमंगलमाङ्गल्या शिवदा शान्तिदा बला।
त्रिनेत्रा रक्तवसना शूलहस्ता कपालिनी॥७॥

गदिनी चक्रिणी शंखी चापिनी बाणधारिणी।
शक्तिधारा खड्गधारा पाशधारा महोदरी॥८॥

अक्षमालाधरा देवी परमा शक्तिरूपिणी।
हंसी हंसगतिर्विद्या मृत्युञ्जयविहारिणी॥९॥

वरा वरेण्या वरदा वरारोहा बलोत्कटा।
जया विजया विमला वन्द्या वन्दारुवत्सला॥१०॥

वागीश्वरी च काम्या कामनाशिन्यमोघदा।
कामचारिणी कामदा कामरूपा प्रभावती॥११॥

इन्द्राणी इन्द्ररूपा च ब्राह्मी ब्रह्मस्वरूपिणी।
वैष्णवी विष्णुरूपा च रुद्राणी रुद्ररूपिणी॥१२॥

माहेश्वरी च माहेन्द्री वाराही नारसिंहिका।
कौमारी कार्तिकेयी च चामुण्डा चण्डवेगिनी॥१३॥

सुभद्रा सुभगा शुद्धा निर्मला परमेश्वरी।
सत्या चैव सरस्वती विश्वामित्रप्रियंकरी॥१४॥

दुर्गा कात्यायनी चैव चन्द्रघण्टा महातपाः।
सावित्री विजयोदारा शाकम्भर्यनघा तथा॥१५

शैलपुत्री सतीश्वरी तारा त्रिपुरसुन्दरी।
भैरवी च मातंगी च धूमावती तथैव च॥१६॥

भुवनेश्वरी बगला छिन्नमस्तापरापरा।
महालक्ष्मीर्महाकाली महासरस्वती शुभा॥१७॥

राजराजेश्वरी चैव राजमातंगिनी परा।
सर्वदेवमयी विद्या सर्वदेवस्वरूपिणी॥१८॥

सर्वशक्तिमयी शक्तिः सर्वसौभाग्यदायिनी।
सर्वविद्याधिदेवी च सर्वव्यापिनी ईश्वरी॥१९॥

कालरात्रिर्महारात्रिर्दिव्यरात्रिर्जयप्रदा।
मधुकैटभहन्त्री च महिषासुरमर्दिनी॥२०॥

धूम्रलोचनहन्त्री च रक्तबीजविनाशिनी।
चण्डमुण्डविनाशा च निशुम्भशुम्भहारिणी॥२१॥

वन्द्या स्तुत्या शुभमया भवभामिनिनाशिनी।
शङ्करार्धशरीरा च विष्णुमाया विमोहिनी॥२२॥

क्षमा तुष्टिः पुष्टिरूपा रतिर्दीप्तिर्धृतिः स्मृतिः।
मेधा स्वधा श्रुतिर्नीतिर्लज्जा ज्योत्स्ना तथा क्षमा॥२३॥

शान्तिः श्रद्धा धृतिः कान्तिर्ज्वाला मातृरूपिणी।
सिद्धिदात्री यशोदा च सर्वरोगनिवारिणी॥२४॥

सहस्रनामैतद्दिव्यं देव्या दुर्गापरात्मनः।
त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नित्यं सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात्॥२५॥

सर्वपापक्षयं याति सर्वशत्रुविनाशनम्।
सम्पत्तिमतुलां प्राप्य मोक्षमन्ते च गच्छति॥२६॥

॥ इति श्री दुर्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Durga Sahasranama Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Durga-sahasranama-stotra-mantrasya Narayana Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Mahadurga Devata.
Shri Durga-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Narayana Uvacha -

Shata-nama-pravakshyami Shrinu Narada Tattvatah.
Sahasranama-sampurnam Durgayah Paramatmanah.

Om Shrim Hrim Dum Durgayai Namah.

Shiva Uma Rama Brahmi Varada Pushkarekshana.
Gayatri Parvati Chaiva Durga Durgarti-nashini.||1||

Jaya Chadya Vishalakshi Muktida Shuddha-rupini.
Mahamaya Mahadevi Mahalakshmi Manonmani.||2||

Mahashaktir Mahotsaha Mahabala Mahaprabha.
Mahabuddhir Mahasiddhir Mahayogishvareshvari.||3||

Mahatantra Mahamantra Mahaayantra Mahasana.
Mahayaga-krama-aradhya Mahabhairava-pujita.||4||

Maheshvari Mahakali Mahagrasa Mahashana.
Aparna Chandika Chanda-mundasura-nishudini.||5||

Mahishasura-nirnashi Bhaktanam Abhaya-prada.
Jayada Buddhida Nitya Sharvani Sharva-vallabha.||6||

Sarva-mangala-mangalya Shivada Shantida Bala.
Trinetra Rakta-vasana Shula-hasta Kapalini.||7||

Gadini Chakrini Shankhi Chapini Bana-dharini.
Shakti-dhara Khadga-dhara Pasha-dhara Mahodari.||8||

Akshamala-dhara Devi Parama Shakti-rupini.
Hamsi Hamsa-gatir Vidya Mrityunjaya-viharini.||9||

Vara Varenya Varada Vara-aroha Balotkata.
Jaya Vijaya Vimala Vandya Vandaru-vatsala.||10||

Vagishvari Cha Kamya Kama-nashiny Amoghada.
Kama-charini Kamada Kama-rupa Prabhavati.||11||

Indrani Indra-rupa Cha Brahmi Brahma-svarupini.
Vaishnavi Vishnu-rupa Cha Rudrani Rudra-rupini.||12||

Maheshvari Cha Mahendri Varahi Narasimhika.
Kaumari Kartikeyi Cha Chamunda Chanda-vegini.||13||

Subhadra Subhaga Shuddha Nirmala Parameshvari.
Satya Chaiva Sarasvati Vishvamitra-priyamkari.||14||

Durga Katyayani Chaiva Chandraghanta Mahatapah.
Savitri Vijayodara Shakambhary Anagha Tatha.||15||

Shaila-putri Satishvari Tara Tripura-sundari.
Bhairavi Cha Matangi Cha Dhumavati Tathaiva Cha.||16||

Bhuvaneshvari Bagala Chhinna-masta-para-apara.
Mahalakshmir Mahakali Mahasarasvati Shubha.||17||

Raja-rajeshvari Chaiva Raja-matangini Para.
Sarva-deva-mayi Vidya Sarva-deva-svarupini.||18||

Sarva-shakti-mayi Shaktih Sarva-saubhagya-dayini.
Sarva-vidyadhi-devi Cha Sarva-vyapini Ishvari.||19||

Kalaratrir Maharatrir Divyaratrir Jaya-prada.
Madhu-kaitabha-hantri Cha Mahishasura-mardini.||20||

Dhumra-lochana-hantri Cha Raktabija-vinashini.
Chanda-munda-vinasha Cha Nishumbha-shumbha-harini.||21||

Vandya Stutya Shubha-maya Bhava-bhamini-nashini.
Shankara-ardha-sharira Cha Vishnu-maya Vimohini.||22||

Kshama Tushtih Pushti-rupa Ratir Diptir Dhritih Smritih.
Medha Svadha Shrutir Nitir Lajja Jyotsna Tatha Kshama.||23||

Shantih Shraddha Dhritih Kantir Jvala Matri-rupini.
Siddhi-datri Yashoda Cha Sarva-roga-nivarini.||24||

Sahasra-namaitad Divyam Devya Durga-paratmanah.
Tri-sandhyam Yah Pathen Nityam Sarvan Kaman Avapnuyat.||25||

Sarva-papa-kshayam Yati Sarva-shatru-vinashanam.
Sampattim Atulam Prapya Moksham Ante Cha Gachchhati.||26||

|| Iti Shri Durga Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नारायण ने कहा - हे नारद! दुर्गा के सहस्र नाम सुनो।

शिवा, उमा, रमा, ब्राह्मी, वरदा, गायत्री, पार्वती, दुर्गा, दुर्गार्तिनाशिनी, जया, विशालाक्षी, मुक्तिदा, शुद्धरूपिणी, महामाया, महादेवी, महालक्ष्मी, महाशक्ति, महाबुद्धि, महासिद्धि।

महेश्वरी, महाकाली, अपर्णा, चण्डिका, चण्डमुण्डासुरनाशिनी, महिषासुरमर्दिनी, भक्तों को अभयदायिनी।

त्रिनेत्रा, कपालिनी, गदिनी, चक्रिणी, शंखिनी, शक्तिधारा, खड्गधारा, पाशधारा। हंसी, विद्या, मृत्युञ्जयविहारिणी।

इन्द्राणी, ब्राह्मी, वैष्णवी, रुद्राणी, माहेश्वरी, वाराही, नारसिंही, चामुण्डा, कौमारी।

शैलपुत्री, तारा, त्रिपुरसुन्दरी, भैरवी, मातंगी, धूमावती, भुवनेश्वरी, बगला, छिन्नमस्ता। दशमहाविद्या सहित सम्पूर्ण नाम।

जो त्रिसन्ध्या इसका पाठ करे, सब काम पूर्ण हों, सब पाप नष्ट हों, शत्रुनाश हो और अन्त में मोक्ष प्राप्त हो।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीदुर्गासहस्रनामस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारायण ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहादुर्गा देवता। श्रीं ह्रीं दुं बीजम्। दुर्गा शक्तिः। श्रीदुर्गाप्रीत्यर्थे सहस्रनामपाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Contains 1000 names of Goddess Durga covering all her cosmic forms
Includes names of all Dasha Mahavidyas and Navadurga forms
Tri-sandhya recitation fulfills all desires and destroys all sins
Invokes unparalleled prosperity and ultimate liberation (moksha)
Comprehensive worship of all Shakti manifestations in one stotram
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and grants fearlessness to devotees
description: The Durga Sahasranama Stotram, known in Sanskrit as दुर्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्, is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names in praise of Goddess Durga. This sacred composition is sourced from Skanda Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to contain 1000 names of Goddess Durga covering all her cosmic forms and grant unparalleled prosperity and ultimate liberation (moksha). It is also said to comprehensive worship of all Shakti manifestations in one stotram. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Diwali to invoke divine grace. At 26 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 35 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Durga Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Durga Sahasranama Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Goddess Durga including Dasha Mahavidya and Navadurga forms.

seoFaqs.question: Where in the scriptures does the Durga Sahasranama Stotram appear?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely recited Durga Sahasranama is drawn from the Skanda Purana, where it appears in a dialogue between Sage Narada and Skanda (Kartikeya). As Drikpanchang and Bhakti Nidhi note, the closing colophon of this version reads 'iti shri-skande purane skanda-narada-samvade durga-sahasranama-stotram sampurnam', explicitly attributing it to that Purana. A second well-known recension is preserved in the Tantraraja Tantra (a Sri Vidya text), as documented in the Scribd edition. The two recensions share a substantial overlap of names but differ in some epithets and verse order, which is why scholarly editions often print them in parallel. The Skanda Purana version is the dominant one in mainstream Devi parayana.

seoFaqs.question: Are there exactly 1000 names or 1008 in the Durga Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard text contains 1000 (sahasra) names organised into shlokas, but several published Sahasranamavali editions extend the count to 1008 by adding eight extra namaskara lines or by counting prefixes such as Sri-Maha. As Bhakti Nidhi explicitly notes in its 'Durga Sahasranamavali in English - 1008 names' edition, both forms circulate side by side. The discrepancy is liturgical rather than doctrinal: 1008 is the standard mala count for japa, so vali (list) editions intended for archana with kumkum or flowers are padded to 1008 names, while the stotram (continuous shloka) format preserves the canonical 1000. Devotees use whichever format matches their puja purpose.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Durga Sahasranama traditionally recited during Navratri?
seoFaqs.answer: During Sharad Navratri, the Durga Sahasranama is one of the principal parayana texts alongside Durga Saptashati. As JKYog India describes, devotees typically chant it once daily for nine days, often after morning sandhya and before commencing Saptashati paath. Larger temples and homes perform an archana version where each name is recited while offering kumkum, flowers or akshata to the Devi pratima. On Ashtami and Navami, many practitioners chant the full Sahasranama 3, 9 or 108 times as part of intensive sadhana. The recitation is preceded by a sankalpa naming the day, tithi and intention, and is closed with the Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram to compensate for any errors.

seoFaqs.question: What kinds of names does the Durga Sahasranama include?
seoFaqs.answer: The 1000 names span Durga's full theological identity: cosmic principles (Adya, Mahamaya, Para Brahmasvarupini), warrior epithets (Mahishasura-mardini, Chandika, Bhairavi), Navadurga forms (Shailaputri, Brahmacharini and so on), Sri Vidya names (Lalita, Tripura, Bhuvaneshwari), and pan-Shakta names (Kali, Tara, Bhagavati, Annapurna). As Stotra Nidhi and Drikpanchang note, this catholic naming is what makes the Sahasranama universally usable across Shakta sub-traditions. The list also includes geographic and ritual epithets such as Vindhyavasini, Kamakhya, Sharada, Vaishnavi, allowing devotees from any region to find their local Devi within the same hymn. The cumulative effect is to assert that all named goddesses are forms of one Durga.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Durga Sahasranama differ from the Durga Saptashati?
seoFaqs.answer: Although both are foundational Devi texts recited together in Navratri, they belong to different scriptural sources and serve different functions. Durga Saptashati (also called Devi Mahatmya or Chandi Path) is a 700-verse narrative from the Markandeya Purana that recounts the Devi's three great battles against Madhu-Kaitabha, Mahishasura and Shumbha-Nishumbha. Durga Sahasranama, drawn from the Skanda Purana, is a non-narrative litany of 1000 names that praises the Devi without telling her stories. As Rudraksha Ratna explains, the Saptashati is closer to scripture-recitation while the Sahasranama is closer to nama-japa. Many Shakta households recite both daily during Navratri, treating them as complementary.

seoFaqs.question: Can one recite only a portion of the Durga Sahasranama on a busy day?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition strongly prefers the complete recitation in one sitting, since the 1000 names form a continuous mantra-body. However, when full recitation is impossible, accepted shorter substitutes include the Durga Ashtottarashata Namavali (108 names), the dhyana shloka with phala shruti (concluding verses) of the Sahasranama, or a single round of the principal beeja mantra. Bhakti Nidhi and Drikpanchang publish both the Sahasranamavali and shorter Ashtottara forms specifically to address this need. Pandits recommend that devotees who genuinely cannot complete the full text on a given day still take sankalpa, recite the dhyana, then chant whatever portion time permits, and ask forgiveness via the Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram for the abridgement.
